<findings>

[observation] [info] High cohesion (0.84) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [info] Guard system co-located with tools ensures security validation stays close to execution
[observation] [info] Highest cohesion (0.84) shows the tool and guard systems form a tight, well-bounded module
[pattern] [info] Guard modules (commands, paths) form a cohesive security layer distinct from tool implementations—candidate for extraction if tooling grows
[anti-pattern] [warning] guard/ subdirectory has 4 files with distinct responsibility (security enforcement) from tools/ (capability provision)—should be separate zones
[suggestion] [warning] Add integration tests for guard subsystem to verify command filtering works correctly with actual shell tool invocations
[suggestion] [info] Audit shell.ts and guard/commands.ts for duplicated command parsing/validation logic

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (0.84) — files are tightly interconnected
- Guard subsystem is critical for safe autonomous execution—maintain high test coverage here
- Tools are well-isolated from agent logic, enabling independent testing and evolution
- Guard subsystem within tooling enforces security boundaries—this is an implicit sub-zone that could be promoted
- Guard subsystem (commands.ts, paths.ts, index.ts) is security-critical and could benefit from being a standalone zone with stricter change review
- Guard subsystem has no integration tests despite being security-critical—only unit tests present
- tools/shell.ts and guard/commands.ts likely overlap in command validation logic

</insights>
